briefdescription.para: Allocates memory for co_sync_t object using allocator from can_net_t.
Frees memory allocated for co_sync_t object.
Initializes co_sync_t object.
Finalizes co_tpdo_t object.
Updates and (de)activates a SYNC producer/consumer service.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked by the download indication functions when one of the SYNC configuration objects (1005, 1006 or 1019) is updated.

briefdescription.para: The download indication function for (all sub-objects of) CANopen object 1005 (COB-ID SYNC message).
The download indication function for (all sub-objects of) CANopen object 1006 (Communication cycle period).
The download indication function for (all sub-objects of) CANopen object 1019 (Synchronous counter overflow value).
The CAN receive callback function for a SYNC consumer service.
The CAN timer callback function for a SYNC producer service.
Returns the alignment (in bytes) of the co_sync_t structure.
Returns the size (in bytes) of the co_sync_t structure.
Creates a new CANopen SYNC producer/consumer service.

detaileddescription.para: The service is stopped after creations and SHOULD be started with co_sync_start() before use.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN network.
a pointer to a CANopen device.

simplesect.para: a pointer to a new SYNC service, or NULL on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Destroys a CANopen SYNC producer/consumer service.
Starts a SYNC service.
Stops a SYNC service.
Returns true if the specified SYNC service is stopped, and false if not.
Returns a pointer to the CAN network of a SYNC producer/consumer service.
Returns a pointer to the CANopen device of a SYNC producer/consumer service.
Retrieves the indication function invoked after a CANopen SYNC message is received or transmitted.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a SYNC consumer service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the indication function invoked after a CANopen SYNC message is received or transmitted.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a SYNC consumer service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to ind.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the error handling function of a SYNC consumer service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a SYNC consumer service.
the address at which to store a pointer to the error handling function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the error handling function of a SYNC consumer service.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a SYNC consumer service.
a pointer to the function to be invoked.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to err.

briefdescription.para: This file is part of the CANopen library; it contains the implementation of the synchronization (SYNC) object functions.

detaileddescription.para:
lely/co/sync.h
